deltas.releaseNoteText:
Added six methods on `CrdtElement` implementing a unified message stream for Yjs-backed collaborative editors. The stream carries both sync and awareness messages over a single Firebase RTDB channel per document, with encryption at rest, ordered serialization, and automatic pruning after snapshot checkpoints.

The six new methods are:
- `pushMessage(query)` — push a lib0-encoded sync or awareness message to the stream
- `onMessage(query)` — subscribe to real-time messages; returns an unsubscribe function
- `getMessages(query)` — fetch all messages after a given timestamp (one-time read for replay)
- `getSnapshot(query)` — retrieve the latest full-state snapshot as a baseline for replay
- `saveSnapshot(query)` — checkpoint the current Y.Doc state and vector clock
- `pruneMessages(query)` — remove messages older than a given timestamp to keep storage bounded
deltas.summary: Create a new rule documenting the six CrdtElement message-stream methods (pushMessage, onMessage, getMessages, getSnapshot, saveSnapshot, pruneMessages) that implement the y-redis synchronization pattern over Firebase RTDB for Yjs-backed collaborative editors.

details.apiName: CrdtElement.pushMessage / onMessage / getMessages / getSnapshot / saveSnapshot / pruneMessages
details.signature:
pushMessage(query: { id: string; data: number[]; yjsClientId: number; messageType: 'sync' | 'awareness'; source?: string }): Promise<void>
onMessage(query: { id: string; callback: (msg: { data: number[] }) => void }): () => void
getMessages(query: { id: string; afterTs: number }): Promise<Array<{ data: number[] }>>
getSnapshot(query: { id: string }): Promise<{ state: number[]; timestamp: number; vector: number[] } | null>
saveSnapshot(query: { id: string; state: number[]; vector: number[]; source?: string }): Promise<void>
pruneMessages(query: { id: string; beforeTs: number }): Promise<void>

parameters.name: query.id
parameters.description: Unique document identifier for the message stream channel

parameters.name: query.data
parameters.description: lib0-encoded Yjs update as a plain number array (pushMessage)

parameters.name: query.yjsClientId
parameters.description: The Y.Doc clientID of the sending peer (pushMessage)

parameters.name: query.messageType
parameters.description: Whether the message carries a document sync update or an awareness update (pushMessage)

parameters.name: query.source
parameters.description: Optional label identifying the editor integration (e.g. 'tiptap') for debugging

parameters.name: query.callback
parameters.description: Invoked for each incoming real-time message (onMessage)

parameters.name: query.afterTs
parameters.description: Unix-ms timestamp; only messages newer than this value are returned (getMessages)

parameters.name: query.state
parameters.description: Full Y.Doc state as produced by Y.encodeStateAsUpdate (saveSnapshot)

parameters.name: query.vector
parameters.description: Y.Doc state vector as produced by Y.encodeStateVector (saveSnapshot)

parameters.name: query.beforeTs
parameters.description: Unix-ms timestamp; messages older than this value are deleted (pruneMessages)

details.returnType: pushMessage → Promise<void>; onMessage → () => void (unsubscribe); getMessages → Promise<Array<{ data: number[] }>>; getSnapshot → Promise<{ state: number[]; timestamp: number; vector: number[] } | null>; saveSnapshot → Promise<void>; pruneMessages → Promise<void>

deltas.releaseNoteText: The `get`, `save`, and `delete` methods on `CommentAnnotationDataProvider`, `ReactionAnnotationDataProvider`, and `AttachmentDataProvider` are now optional. Developers using config-based URL endpoints (`config.getConfig`, `config.saveConfig`, `config.deleteConfig`) no longer need to supply placeholder callbacks. `ResolverConfig` also gains a new `additionalFields?: string[]` property for including custom fields in resolver payloads.
deltas.summary: Create a new rule documenting CommentAnnotationDataProvider (and the parallel ReactionAnnotationDataProvider and AttachmentDataProvider) — covering the fact that get/save/delete callbacks are now optional when using config-based URL endpoints, the ResolverConfig.additionalFields property, and the two valid registration forms (callback-based vs. config-based).

parameters.name: get
parameters.description: Callback to fetch a comment annotation from your backend. Optional when config.getConfig is provided.

parameters.name: save
parameters.description: Callback to persist a comment annotation to your backend. Optional when config.saveConfig is provided.

parameters.name: delete
parameters.description: Callback to delete a comment annotation from your backend. Optional when config.deleteConfig is provided.

parameters.name: config.additionalFields
parameters.description: Extra fields to include in the resolver payload sent to config-based HTTP endpoints.

details.returnType: N/A — provider object passed to setDataProviders()
